query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PTFE that can be used in industrial seals applications

properties: Excellent chemical resistance to acids, bases, organic solvents
Low permeability to gases and liquids
Good mechanical strength (tensile >50 MPa, modulus >3 GPa)
Acceptable friction coefficient (<0.4) for seal operation
constraints: Must be manufacturable at laboratory scale (extrusion or injection molding)
Cost and availability suitable for lab prototyping
No requirement for radiation sterilization compatibility
Material must not contain fluorine atoms in the backbone

final_candidate.justification: PEEK 450G offers a melt temperature of ~350 °C, allowing processing up to 260 °C in service, matching PTFE’s high‑temperature capability. It has superior mechanical strength (tensile 55–65 MPa, modulus 3.5–4 GPa) and excellent chemical resistance to most acids, bases, and organic solvents, making it suitable for aggressive industrial seal environments. The material is non‑PFAS, eliminating fluorine content while providing low permeability (<10⁻¹⁰ m³/m·s). VICTREX™ PEEK 450G is commercially available in granule form, widely used in lab‑scale injection molding and extrusion, and has documented use in high‑temperature sealing applications.

process_recipe.description: Dry PEEK granules to remove moisture before processing.
process_recipe.conditions: 120 °C in a convection oven for 2 h (≤5 % moisture content).

process_recipe.description: Extrude dried PEEK to produce filament or preform for molding.
process_recipe.conditions: Twin‑screw extruder barrel zones set to 350–360 °C, screw speed 50 rpm, feed rate 10 kg/h.

process_recipe.description: Cool extrudate in a water bath and pelletize for injection molding.

process_recipe.conditions: Mold temperature 120–140 °C, melt temperature 360 °C, injection pressure 200 bar, hold time 5 min, cooling 10 min.

process_recipe.description: Optional post‑cure to relieve residual stresses.
